last_item.headline: Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick speaks to media in Darwin
last_item.body: Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick defends the club not standing down a staff member who is being investigated by the AFL integrity unit.

last_item.headline: Voss says Carlton in a better place than when he took charge
last_item.body: Michael Voss has struck a defiant tone after leaving Carlton, insistent the AFL club is in better shape than when he started as coach.

last_item.headline: Carlton interim coach Fraser pulls out of running for full-time role
last_item.body: Josh Fraser will lead Carlton for the remainder of the season after Michael Voss's exit, but isn't prepared to do the job full-time just yet.

last_item.headline: Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick speaks to media in Darwin
last_item.body: Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick defends the club not standing down a staff member who is being investigated by the AFL integrity unit.

last_item.headline: Swans move annual Pride Game away from St Kilda after Collard case
last_item.body: The fallout continues from the Lance Collard AFL tribunal case, with St Kilda cut from the annual Pride Game against Sydney.

last_item.headline: Voss says Carlton in a better place than when he took charge
last_item.body: Michael Voss has struck a defiant tone after leaving Carlton, insistent the AFL club is in better shape than when he started as coach.

last_item.headline: Rankine says he has 'come a long way' since homophobic slur ban
last_item.body: After being forced to sit out of last year's finals series thanks to a suspension for a homophobic slur, Izak Rankine is confident he has grown.

last_item.headline: 'Relief' as Voss resigns as Carlton coach after torrid run of results
last_item.body: Michael Voss has left Carlton, resigning with immediate effect following a run of seven consecutive AFL defeats, with another former Carlton boss Mick Malthouse saying the Brisbane premiership-winning player will be relieved to be out of the hot seat.

last_item.headline: Crows footballer tells fans to 'choose kindness' after social media abuse
last_item.body: Adelaide Crows footballer Josh Rachele has called out fan behaviour after receiving an abusive message online amid an ongoing conversation on mental health in the AFL.

last_item.headline: Women's players sick of being told 'don't bite the hand that feeds you'
last_item.body: Players from various codes across the country will protest gender-based violence and call for greater accountability from clubs when instances of mistreatment arise.

last_item.headline: ‘BS s–t wrestling drills’: Cornes slams Crows as young star suffers serious knee injury in training mishap
last_item.body: Adelaide young gun Dan Curtin is facing a delayed start to the 2026 season, after suffering a suspected dislocated kneecap in a training incident.Curtin was stretchered from the ground at the Crows’ West Lakes facility after sustaining the injury in a wrestling drill on Monday, with club doctors wrenching his kneecap back into place.According to reports, the 20-year old’s anterior cruciate ligamen

last_item.headline: The unsigned guns who could be next year’s Callum Ah Chee in a new era of pre-season draft recruiting
last_item.body: A few weeks ago, Adelaide and Callum Ah Chee exploited the pre-season draft to engineer a de facto free agency move to facilitate his move from Brisbane, after a trade fell through.The ordinary way the PSD has been used for the last 20 years has been for teams to use it to pick up marginal players who fell out of their previous teams, or to move their own players around their lists.Ah Chee used it

last_item.headline: The battle for Tasmania's north has been won, but locals aren't convinced
last_item.body: They may not be scheduled to play their first AFL game until 2028, but the Tasmania Devils have claimed their first scalp — booting Hawthorn out of the state which has been its second home for more than two decades.
